mapping.a_element: overdamped diffusion processes driven out of thermal equilibrium
mapping.b_element: stochastic differential equations with a new interpretation specifying dynamics

mapping.a_element: thermodynamic interpretation of joint fluctuations of occupation times and currents
mapping.b_element: built-in Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution in the new interpretation of SDE

mapping.a_element: canonical structure of joint fluctuations (time-symmetric and time-antisymmetric sectors)
mapping.b_element: potential function revealing local and global dynamics in the new interpretation

mapping.a_element: decoupling of occupation and current fluctuations close-to-equilibrium
mapping.b_element: relation between the new interpretation and Ito process (classical equilibrium-like behavior)

brief_justification: Both papers address the structure of fluctuations in stochastic processes, with Paper A focusing on driven diffusions and thermodynamic sectors, while Paper B introduces a new SDE interpretation with a built-in Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution and potential function. The shared machinery lies in the equilibrium/non-equilibrium statistical framework, where occupation statistics and potential functions play analogous roles in describing steady-state dynamics.
